commands.review_summary:
This PR accepts successful no-op preflight compaction results, forwards the reply-path context budget into compaction, clamps runtime compaction budgets to the selected compaction model, and adds focused unit coverage.

PR surface: Source +22, Tests +71. Total +93 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: Source-level yes: current main requires both `ok` and `compacted` before continuing, so an `ok: true, compacted: false` result fails preflight. I did not execute tests in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane: source review found no actionable patch defect, and the remaining work is maintainer/CI validation for a protected maintainer PR.

target.title: Fix preflight compaction no-op budget handling
target.body:
## Summary

- Treat `ok: true, compacted: false` preflight compaction results as successful no-ops instead of failing the reply path.
- Pass the caller-resolved context budget from `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts` into runtime compaction so the compactor evaluates the same budget that triggered preflight.
- Clamp caller-provided compaction budgets to the selected compaction model's resolved context window in `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/compact.ts`, so an active conversation model cannot overstate the capacity of a smaller compaction model.
- Added focused coverage for no-op preflight behavior, budget forwarding, and compaction-model budget clamping.

LOC delta against `origin/main`: `+100 / -7`, net `+93` LOC.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`

Focused Vitest cases were attempted, but local runs stayed CPU-bound with no output both sandboxed and unsandboxed. They were stopped with exit `143` to avoid leaving stuck processes behind.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: preflight compaction no longer fails a successful no-op result, while runtime compaction still receives and clamps the caller budget safely.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/preflight-compaction-noop`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git diff --check`; `pnpm tsgo:core`.

Evidence after fix: whitespace check passed; core TypeScript lane passed.

Observed result after fix: branch typechecks with the new budget-forwarding and budget-clamping paths.

What was not tested: focused Vitest completion; local Vitest processes remained CPU-bound with no output and were killed after bounded waits.
